content.problemStatement:
There are initially XXX people in a room.

You are given an array AAA of length NNN which describes the following events:


If Ai≥0A_i \geq 0Ai​≥0, then AiA_iAi​ people enter the room at iii-th minute. For e.g. if A2=3A_2 = 3A2​=3, then 333 people enter the room at the 222-nd minute.
If Ai<0A_i \lt 0Ai​<0, then ∣Ai∣|A_i|∣Ai​∣ people leave the room at iii-th minute. Here ∣Ai∣|A_i|∣Ai​∣ denotes the absolute value of AiA_iAi​. For e.g. if A4=−2A_4 = -2A4​=−2, then 222 people leave the room at the 444-th minute.
Determine the maximum number of people in the room at any moment of time.

It is guaranteed in the input that at any moment of time, the number of people in the room does not become negative.


Input Format

The first line will contain TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case consists of two integers NNN and XXX - the length of the array AAA and the number of people in the room initially.
The second line of each test case contains NNN integers A1,A2,A3,…ANA_1, A_2, A_3, \dots A_NA1​,A2​,A3​,…AN​.


Output Format
For each testcase, output the maximum number of people in the room at any point of time.
constraints: The number of people never becomes negative.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains two integers N and X — the length of the array and the initial number of people in the room. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN describing the events.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer — the maximum number of people that are present in the room at any moment, each on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: sequence of people = 8,8,11,14,1,6 → maximum 14.\nTest case 2: 5,5,3,5,8 → maximum 8.\nTest case 3: 5,3,8,6 → maximum 8.

solution.approach: Maintain two variables: cur = X (current number of people) and ans = X (maximum seen so far). Scan the array from i=1 to N, update cur += Ai and set ans = max(ans, cur). Because the input guarantees cur never drops below zero, no extra checks are needed. After processing the whole array, ans is the required maximum.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T·N) overall.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = list(map(int, sys.stdin.read().strip().split()))
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = next(it)
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = next(it); X = next(it)
        cur = X
        ans = X
        for _ in range(N):
            a = next(it)
            cur += a
            if cur > ans:
                ans = cur
        out.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
